reuseStrategy: Content fragments are identified based on cross-page reuse. Global fragments (header, footer) are shared across all 321 pages. Section-level fragments are shared across template groups.

fragments.name: Global Header Navigation
fragments.description: Complete header with AbbVie logo, 5-item mega-menu navigation, search input, and region/country selector.

structure.regionSelector: Globe icon → 50+ country links by continent

fragments.contentModel: Navigation tree with 3 levels of hierarchy

fragments.name: Global Footer
fragments.description: Multi-column footer with logo, section navigation, social media icons, popular pages, external links, legal bar, and copyright.
fragments.contentModel: Static content fragment with link collections

fragments.name: External Link Warning Modal
fragments.description: Modal dialog warning users they are leaving AbbVie website, with agree/disagree buttons.

structure.title: You are about to leave the AbbVie website

fragments.reusePages: All pages (triggered by JS on external link clicks)

fragments.name: Cookie Consent Banner
fragments.description: Privacy cookie banner with consent description and settings button. Appears on first visit.

structure.description: Privacy notice about cookies and tracking

fragments.name: Story Related Content Section
fragments.description: 3-column card grid of related stories shown at the bottom of article pages. Content varies by article category.
fragments.contentModel: Dynamic: queried by article category tag

fragments.name: Article Metadata Fragment
fragments.description: Reusable metadata bar pattern (date + category + read time) used on all article pages.

fragments.name: Region/Country Selector
fragments.description: Country/region chooser organized by continent. 50+ country links to localized AbbVie sites.

fragments.name: Social Media Links
fragments.description: Collection of 6 social media platform links with branded icons.

summary.totalFragmentInstances: 321 pages × 4 global + 116 articles × 2 article-specific = 1,516 fragment instances
